message: React Hook "useFullRender" cannot be called in an async function.
font-bold on <h1> crushes counter shapes at display sizes — use font-semibold (600) or font-medium (500)
help: Use `font-semibold` (600) or `font-medium` (500) on headings — 700+ crushes letter counter shapes at display sizes

message: .filter().map() iterates the array twice — combine into a single loop with .reduce() or for...of
help: Combine `.map().filter()` (or similar chains) into a single pass with `.reduce()` or a `for...of` loop to avoid iterating the array twice

message: Pure black background (bg-black) looks harsh — use a near-black tinted toward your brand hue (e.g. bg-gray-950)
help: Tint the background slightly toward your brand hue — e.g. `#0a0a0f` or Tailwind's `bg-gray-950`. Pure black looks harsh on modern displays

message: The `autoFocus` attribute is found here, which can cause usability issues for sighted and non-sighted users.
help: Remove the `autoFocus` attribute.

message: The `autoFocus` attribute is found here, which can cause usability issues for sighted and non-sighted users.
help: Remove the `autoFocus` attribute.

message: Component "WebCommenting" is 384 lines — consider breaking it into smaller focused components
help: Extract logical sections into focused components: `<UserHeader />`, `<UserActions />`, etc.

message: Three-period ellipsis ("...") in JSX text — use the actual ellipsis character "…" (or `…`)
help: Use the typographic ellipsis "…" (or `…`) instead of three periods — pairs with action-with-followup labels ("Rename…", "Loading…")

message: useState "mode" is updated but never read in the component's return — use useRef so updates don't trigger re-renders
help: Replace useState with useRef when the value is only mutated and never read in render — `ref.current = ...` updates without re-rendering the component

message: w-16 h-16 → use the shorthand size-16 (Tailwind v3.4+)
help: Collapse `w-N h-N` to `size-N` (Tailwind v3.4+) when both axes match

message: w-8 h-8 → use the shorthand size-8 (Tailwind v3.4+)
help: Collapse `w-N h-N` to `size-N` (Tailwind v3.4+) when both axes match

message: space-y-2 on a flex/grid parent — use gap-y-2 instead. Per-sibling margins phantom-gap on conditional render and don't mirror in RTL
help: Use `gap-*` on the flex/grid parent. `space-x-*` / `space-y-*` produce phantom gaps when a sibling is conditionally rendered, lose vertical spacing on wrapped lines, and don't mirror in RTL

message: useMutation without a cache update — stale data may remain after the mutation. Call queryClient.invalidateQueries / setQueryData / resetQueries / refetchQueries inside onSuccess (or trigger a router refresh)
help: Add `onSuccess: () => queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['...'] })` so cached data stays in sync after the mutation

message: Sequential `await` without a data dependency on the previous result — wrap the independent calls in `Promise.all([...])` so they race instead of waterfalling
help: Wrap independent awaits in `Promise.all([...])` so they race instead of waterfalling — second call doesn't depend on the first

message: await inside a for…of loop runs the calls sequentially — for independent operations, collect them and use `await Promise.all(items.map(...))` to run them concurrently
help: Collect the items and use `await Promise.all(items.map(...))` to run independent operations concurrently

message: array.includes() in a loop is O(n) per call — convert to a Set for O(1) lookups
help: Use a `Set` or `Map` for repeated membership tests / keyed lookups — `Array.includes`/`find` is O(n) per call

message: 3 sequential await statements that appear independent — use Promise.all() for parallel execution
help: Use `const [a, b] = await Promise.all([fetchA(), fetchB()])` to run independent operations concurrently

help: Use at least 12px for body content, 16px is ideal. Small text is hard to read, especially on high-DPI mobile screens

message: px-4 py-4 → use the shorthand p-4

message: await blocks the function before an early-return that doesn't use the awaited value — move the await after the synchronous guard so the skip path stays fast
help: Move the `await` after the synchronous early-return guard so the skip path stays fast

message: useContext is superseded by `use()` on React 19+ — `use()` reads context conditionally inside hooks, branches, and loops; switch to `import { use } from 'react'`
help: Pass `ref` as a regular prop on function components — `forwardRef` is no longer needed in React 19+. Replace `useContext(X)` with `use(X)` for branch-aware context reads. Only enabled on projects detected as React 19+.

message: 3 setState calls in a single useEffect — consider using useReducer or deriving state
help: Combine into useReducer: `const [state, dispatch] = useReducer(reducer, initialState)`

message: Mutable global "location.*" in deps — values like `location.pathname` can change without triggering a re-render, so they can't drive effect re-runs. Subscribe with useSyncExternalStore or read inside the effect
help: Read mutable values (`location.pathname`, `ref.current`) inside the effect body instead of in the deps array, or subscribe with `useSyncExternalStore`. Mutations to these don't trigger re-renders, so listing them in deps doesn't make the effect react to changes

message: react-dom/test-utils is removed in React 19. Use `import { act } from 'react'` instead.
help: Switch the legacy `react-dom` root API (`render` / `hydrate` / `unmountComponentAtNode`) to `createRoot` / `hydrateRoot` / `root.unmount()` from `react-dom/client`. Replace `findDOMNode` with a ref. The whole `react-dom/test-utils` entry point is removed in React 19 — use `act` from `react` and `fireEvent` / `render` from `@testing-library/react`. Only enabled on projects detected as React 18+.
